task: I need help writing a short message to a teacher about an assignment issue. Can you give me a draft that gets the point across clearly and stays easy to send?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, easy-to-send draft:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I just wanted to ask about one part of the assignment. I noticed an issue and wanted to check on it with you. Thanks!**

If you want, I can also make it:
- more formal
- more casual
- about a missing grade, late work, or a specific mistake

user_simulator.text:
Close, but it’s too vague. I need it to actually say there’s an issue with the assignment, not just “one part.” Something short like:

Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name]. I wanted to check with you before I turn anything in / so I can fix it. Thanks.

If you want, make it a little more specific without making it long.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a slightly more specific version that still stays short:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name]. I wanted to check with you before I turn it in so I can make sure it’s correct. Thanks!**

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more direct
- more casual
- specific to a mistake, missing work, or grade issue

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close. Still a little too generic though — “make sure it’s correct” sounds kind of soft. I want it to actually say I need to fix something or that there’s an issue with the assignment, just without making it long.

Maybe something like:

Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name], and I wanted to check before I turn it in so I can fix anything that needs fixing. Thanks.

Can you make it a tiny bit sharper, like for a missing part or wrong instructions?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a sharper version that still stays short:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name], and I wanted to check before I turn it in so I can fix anything that needs fixing. Thanks.**

If you want it a little more direct for a missing part or wrong instructions, you could use:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name]—it looks like something may be missing or unclear. I wanted to check before I turn it in so I can fix it. Thanks.**

If you want, I can make it:
- more specific about missing work
- about wrong instructions
- a little more formal or casual

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, the second one is closer. Still a little soft though — “looks like something may be missing or unclear” sounds kinda hesitant.

I’d want it more like:

Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name] — it looks like a part is missing / the instructions don’t match. I wanted to check before I turn it in so I can fix it. Thanks.

If you can, make it sound a little cleaner than that but keep the same meaning.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a cleaner version with the same meaning:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I noticed there may be an issue with [assignment name] — it looks like a part is missing or the instructions don’t match. I wanted to check before I turn it in so I can fix it. Thanks.**

If you want, I can make it:
- a little more casual
- more direct
- specific to missing work or wrong instructions

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Cleaner than my version, and it still says the issue is there without sounding weirdly dramatic.
